user_message: Working on Azure Ridge Partners's QBR preparation. Got the email thread -- help me prepare the forecast.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Thomas Okafor <thomas.okafor@azureridgepa.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-01 17:34 EST
Subject: URGENT: QBR preparation Issue at Azure Ridge Partners

Our team encountered some challenges with QBR preparation. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Thomas Okafor <thomas.okafor@azureridgepa.com>
Date: 2024-01-16 15:02 EST
Subject: QBR preparation - Action Required

There's been a change in our timeline for QBR preparation. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Azure Ridge Partners: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF37-J trap (Confuses planned maintenance with unplanned outage); produces complete Severity triage, root-cause hypothesis (labeled as hypothesis), customer response draft, ACR risk fl
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF37-J failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF37-J failure mode (Confuses planned maintenance with unplanned outage)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF37-J -- Confuses planned maintenance with unplanned outage
